name: EU Charter of Fundamental Rights (Charter of Nice / EU CFR 2000)
summary: The EU Charter of Fundamental Rights (popularly the EU CFR / formally Charter of Fundamental Rights of the European Union) is a European Union supranational meta-framework EU bloc primary-law human-rights instrument adopted by the European Parliament + Council + Commission — structurally distinctive globally as the only modern supranational meta-framework operating EU bloc primary-law human-rights framework (EU Charter of Fundamental Rights adopted 7 December 2000 in Nice + entered force 1 December 2009 alongside Treaty of Lisbon framework — formal EU bloc primary-law human-rights framework with formal binding legal effect on EU member states when implementing EU law, distinct from ECHR framework which operates as Council of Europe regional framework + UN human-rights frameworks which operate as universal multilateral frameworks, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern supranational meta-framework operating EU bloc primary-law human-rights framework with formal primacy over EU member state domestic law when implementing EU law), as the central jurisdiction of the formal CFR Article 7 (Respect for Private and Family Life) + Article 24 (Rights of the Child) framework (CFR Article 7 framework on respect for private and family life + Article 24 framework on rights of the child — formal EU bloc primary-law family-rights + children's-rights dual framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern supranational meta-framework operating EU bloc primary-law family-rights + children's-rights dual framework with formal binding legal effect), as the central jurisdiction of the formal Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) Charter case-law framework (CJEU Charter case-law framework via formal CJEU jurisdiction over EU Charter framework including landmark cases C-540/03 Parliament v Council 2006 + C-491/10 PPU Aguirre Zarraga 2010 + C-498/14 PPU Bradbrooke 2015 + multiple subsequent cases — formal CJEU Charter case-law framework, structurally distinctive globally as the most-developed modern supranational meta-framework CJEU case-law framework on EU bloc primary-law human-rights framework), and as the central jurisdiction of the formal post-Lisbon Treaty CFR entry-into-force framework (CFR adopted 7 December 2000 + entered force 1 December 2009 alongside Treaty of Lisbon framework — formal post-Lisbon Treaty CFR entry-into-force framework, structurally distinctive globally as the only modern supranational meta-framework that became binding via Lisbon Treaty framework). The CFR is binding upon all 27 EU member states when implementing EU law. The CFR is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label, though formal Article 7 + Article 24 frameworks operate substantial intersection with parental-alienation framework via EU Brussels IIa Regulation 2201/2003 + EU Council Regulation 2019/1111 framework. The Court of Justice of the European Union is the apex judicial body framework; national-level apex courts also operate as judicial bodies via formal preliminary reference framework. The EU Charter of Fundamental Rights is silent on 'parental alienation' as a statutory label.

statutory_framework.citation: EU Charter of Fundamental Rights 2000 (Charter of Nice)
statutory_framework.title: EU Charter of Fundamental Rights
statutory_framework.relevance: EU Charter of Fundamental Rights adopted 7 December 2000 in Nice + entered force 1 December 2009 alongside Treaty of Lisbon framework — formal EU bloc primary-law human-rights framework with formal binding legal effect on EU member states when implementing EU law.

statutory_framework.citation: EU CFR Article 7 (Respect for Private and Family Life) + Article 24 (Rights of the Child)
statutory_framework.title: EU CFR Articles 7 + 24
statutory_framework.relevance: EU CFR Article 7 framework on respect for private and family life + Article 24 framework on rights of the child — formal EU bloc primary-law family-rights + children's-rights dual framework.

statutory_framework.title: Treaty of Lisbon
statutory_framework.relevance: Treaty of Lisbon adopted 13 December 2007 + entered force 1 December 2009 — formal EU bloc treaty framework underlying CFR entry-into-force framework.

statutory_framework.citation: CJEU CFR Case-Law Framework (Parliament v Council 2006 + Aguirre Zarraga 2010 + Bradbrooke 2015 + others)
statutory_framework.title: CJEU CFR Case-Law Framework
statutory_framework.relevance: CJEU Charter case-law framework via formal CJEU jurisdiction over EU Charter framework — formal CJEU Charter case-law framework on EU bloc primary-law human-rights framework.

statutory_framework.citation: EU Brussels IIa Regulation 2201/2003 + EU Council Regulation 2019/1111
statutory_framework.title: EU Brussels IIa Regulation
statutory_framework.relevance: EU Brussels IIa Regulation 2201/2003 + EU Council Regulation 2019/1111 — formal EU bloc family-law framework operating in conjunction with CFR Article 7 + Article 24 frameworks.

apex_courts.name: Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU)
apex_courts.role: Apex judicial body framework for EU Charter of Fundamental Rights.

professional_regulators.name: European Commission (DG JUST)
professional_regulators.role: EU Charter of Fundamental Rights implementation framework.

anonymisation_convention: EU CFR-related individual communications are anonymised per CJEU practice using initials.

key_developments.title: Treaty of Rome + EEC founded
key_developments.description: Treaty of Rome signed 25 March 1957 + European Economic Community EEC founded — beginning of formal EU bloc regional supranational framework.

key_developments.title: Treaty on European Union + EU founded
key_developments.description: Treaty on European Union TEU adopted 7 February 1992 + entered force 1 November 1993 — formal European Union EU established.

key_developments.title: EU Charter of Fundamental Rights adopted
key_developments.description: EU Charter of Fundamental Rights adopted 7 December 2000 in Nice by European Parliament + Council + Commission — formal EU bloc human-rights framework.

key_developments.title: Treaty of Lisbon adopted
key_developments.description: Treaty of Lisbon adopted 13 December 2007 — formal EU bloc treaty framework underlying CFR entry-into-force framework.

key_developments.title: Treaty of Lisbon + CFR enter force
key_developments.description: Treaty of Lisbon entered force 1 December 2009 + EU Charter of Fundamental Rights gained formal binding legal effect alongside Lisbon framework — formal EU bloc primary-law human-rights framework operational.

key_developments.title: Aguirre Zarraga case
key_developments.description: CJEU C-491/10 PPU Aguirre Zarraga Judgment 22 December 2010 — landmark CFR Article 24 case-law framework on cross-border-child-abduction + Brussels IIa Regulation framework.

key_developments.title: EU Council Regulation 2019/1111 enters force
key_developments.description: EU Council Regulation 2019/1111 of 25 June 2019 entered force 1 August 2022 — revised EU Brussels IIa Regulation framework operating in conjunction with CFR Article 7 + Article 24 frameworks.

structural_findings: EU Charter of Fundamental Rights operates a supranational EU bloc primary-law human-rights framework — places EU CFR in the European Union supranational-meta-framework cluster.

Only modern supranational meta-framework operating EU bloc primary-law human-rights framework is structurally distinctive globally — formal EU bloc primary-law human-rights framework with formal primacy over EU member state domestic law when implementing EU law.

Central jurisdiction of formal CFR Article 7 + Article 24 framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern supranational meta-framework operating EU bloc primary-law family-rights + children's-rights dual framework with formal binding legal effect.

Central jurisdiction of formal Court of Justice of the European Union CFR case-law framework is structurally distinctive globally — most-developed modern supranational meta-framework CJEU case-law framework on EU bloc primary-law human-rights framework.

Central jurisdiction of formal post-Lisbon Treaty CFR entry-into-force framework is structurally distinctive globally — only modern supranational meta-framework that became binding via Lisbon Treaty framework.
All 27 EU member state binding framework when implementing EU law is structurally distinctive globally — formal primacy framework over EU member state domestic law.
1957 Treaty of Rome + 1992 TEU + 2000 CFR + 2007 Lisbon Treaty + 2009 entry into force EU-bloc-framework-development sequence is structurally distinctive globally.

CFR Article 7 + Article 24 + EU Brussels IIa Regulation 2201/2003 + EU Council Regulation 2019/1111 EU-bloc-family-law-framework operating-in-conjunction-with-CFR-framework is structurally distinctive globally.

sources.title: European Union — EU Charter of Fundamental Rights
Court of Justice of the European Union

editorial_notes: EU Charter of Fundamental Rights jurisdiction sidecar — supranational European Union EU bloc primary-law human-rights meta-framework (EU CFR 2000/2009 + Article 7 Respect for Private and Family Life + Article 24 Rights of the Child + Treaty of Lisbon 2007/2009 + CJEU CFR Case-Law Framework + EU Brussels IIa Regulation 2201/2003 + EU Council Regulation 2019/1111). Only modern supranational meta-framework operating EU bloc primary-law human-rights framework globally + central jurisdiction of formal CFR Article 7 + Article 24 framework + central jurisdiction of formal Court of Justice of the European Union CFR case-law framework + central jurisdiction of formal post-Lisbon Treaty CFR entry-into-force framework + All 27 EU member state binding framework when implementing EU law + 1957 Treaty of Rome + 1992 TEU + 2000 CFR + 2007 Lisbon Treaty + 2009 entry into force EU-bloc-framework-development sequence + CFR Article 7 + Article 24 + EU Brussels IIa Regulation 2201/2003 + EU Council Regulation 2019/1111 EU-bloc-family-law-framework operating-in-conjunction-with-CFR-framework.
PA-recognition: silent statutory + no-apex-position + silent regulator.
